description_long: This is a large, rectangular police station building with a flat, concrete roof and a utilitarian, modernist design. The structure features a prominent rooftop area with two smaller auxiliary buildings, one of which has a gabled roof and visible windows, while the other is box-shaped and windowless. The main facade is characterized by horizontal striping in dark and light gray tones, with some exposed piping and utility elements along one side. The exterior walls are mostly solid, with minimal decorative features, and the building appears to have multiple entry points, though doors are not clearly visible. The rooftop is expansive and mostly bare, except for the auxiliary structures and some utility installations. The overall appearance is functional and institutional, typical of public service buildings in urban environments.
description: A large, flat-roofed police station with a modern, utilitarian design, featuring auxiliary rooftop structures and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: This view shows the rooftop with two auxiliary structures, exposed piping along the left side, and the layered horizontal facade.
description_view_1: The main rooftop building with a gabled roof is prominent, and the facade displays horizontal striping with some utility features visible.
description_view_2: The auxiliary rooftop structures are visible, with the gabled building and box-shaped structure, and the facade appears mostly solid and unadorned.
description_view_3: The rooftop is clearly visible with both auxiliary structures, and the exposed piping and utility elements are prominent along the edge.
attribute_building_facade_features: Horizontal striping, flat roof, exposed piping, auxiliary rooftop structures, minimal windows, and solid exterior walls.
